instincts.title: Do not rely on LSP diagnostics for typing status
instincts.trigger: When tempted to check typing errors via editor/LSP or `lsp_diagnostics`
instincts.action: Use `uv run basedpyright` as the source of truth; treat LSP output as advisory only.

instincts.title: Do not ship editor/agent tool artifacts in templates
instincts.trigger: When adding or reviewing files under template trees like src/agent_loom/compound/opencode/ (or other scaffolded outputs).
instincts.action: Exclude tool-specific project files (e.g., `.serena/*`, local IDE metadata, assistant-runner configs) from templates and committed scaffolds unless they are a deliberate product feature; prefer removing them rather than ignoring via template-local `.gitignore`.

instincts.title: CLI UX Changes Need Targeted UX Tests
instincts.trigger: When modifying CLI output, help text, defaults, or command structure
instincts.action: Update/extend UX-focused tests to lock in the intended ergonomics and prevent regressions; avoid untested copy changes that silently degrade UX.

instincts.title: CLI UX Changes Need UX Tests
instincts.trigger: When you change CLI text output, flags, or exit-code behavior
instincts.action: Add/adjust a dedicated UX test that asserts exit code + key stdout/stderr lines for the affected command(s); keep assertions minimal but intentional (user-visible contracts only) so refactors don't cause noise.

instincts.title: Add CLI UX regression tests when output changes
instincts.trigger: When changing any CLI command output, flags, exit codes, or user-facing error messages (especially in ticket commands).
instincts.action: Add/extend a pytest UX test that exercises the command and asserts: exit code, stdout/stderr content (including key lines and spacing), and stable formatting. Prefer small, readable golden strings (dedent) over broad regexes; avoid asserting volatile data (timestamps, temp paths) unless you control/freeze it.

instincts.title: Keep instincts docs and compiled JSON in sync
instincts.trigger: When adding or editing any Compound instinct.
instincts.action: Update `.loom/compound/INSTINCTS.md` and regenerate/adjust `.loom/compound/instincts.json` in the same change. Verify IDs are unique, kebab-case, and identical across doc + JSON; ensure any new instinct has a clear trigger/action and minimal tags.

instincts.title: Keep compound install outputs deterministic
instincts.trigger: When changing compound scaffold/template contents or compound install logic (e.g., src/agent_loom/compound/scaffold.py, src/agent_loom/compound/paths.py, src/agent_loom/compound/docs.py, compound opencode template tree).
instincts.action: Treat the installed `.opencode/` (and any `.loom/compound/` docs) as a contract: preserve stable paths, stable file ordering, stable newline formatting, and stable rendered content; immediately update/extend `tests/test_compound_install.py` to lock the behavior.

instincts.title: Keep CLI output deterministic by default
instincts.trigger: When a CLI command prints lists, tables, multi-item sections, or derived metadata (IDs, paths, counts).
instincts.action: Ensure ordering is explicit and stable (sort inputs, stable iteration, deterministic grouping). Normalize or avoid non-deterministic content (timestamps, random IDs). Make newline behavior consistent (exactly one trailing newline; no extra blank lines unless intentional) and cover with a UX test.

instincts.title: Avoid backcompat framing in greenfield code/docs
instincts.trigger: Writing docs, flags, deprecations, or migration behaviors in early-stage systems
instincts.action: Prefer clean breaks and simple interfaces; remove/avoid 'backcompat' language unless there is a real external user contract that requires it.

instincts.title: Align Core Errors With CLI UX
instincts.trigger: When updating core behavior that the CLI surfaces (validation, missing resources, ambiguous inputs)
instincts.action: Make core raise/return a single, well-typed/consistent error shape that CLI maps to stable user-facing messages + non-zero exit codes; add a UX test covering the error path.

instincts.title: Keep OpenAPI and Dashboard Consumers in Sync
instincts.trigger: When changing server routes, request/response shapes, or endpoint behavior
instincts.action: Update `docs/openapi.yaml` in the same change and verify any dashboard readers/clients that depend on those endpoints still work; treat schema drift as a bug.

instincts.title: Update CLI UX tests alongside CLI behavior changes
instincts.trigger: Any change to CLI output, flags, or subcommand routing
instincts.action: Locate and update the UX snapshot/expectation tests (e.g. `tests/test_*_cli_ux.py`) in the same change; ensure wording is intentional and stable.

instincts.title: Keep OpenAPI spec deterministic and non-generated where possible
instincts.trigger: Editing `docs/openapi.yaml` during endpoint/schema updates
instincts.action: Avoid volatile fields like timestamps; keep component and path ordering stable; validate references and schema names stay consistent with the code.

instincts.title: Run Quality Gates Immediately After Refactors
instincts.trigger: After a broad refactor that touches many files and tests
instincts.action: Run `uv run ruff check .`, `uv run basedpyright`, then `uv run pytest`; treat failures as part of the refactor (not follow-up work).

instincts.title: Prefer workflow command docs over duplicating skills in templates
instincts.trigger: When documenting workflows for end-users inside compound-installed `.opencode/` trees.
instincts.action: Put loom command guidance in `.opencode/commands/loom-*.md` (command-discoverable docs) and avoid duplicating full skill content inside the compound template unless required; keep skills procedural and source-of-truth in `.opencode/skills/` at repo root.

instincts.title: Run typecheck + lint gates before pytest
instincts.trigger: After non-trivial Python edits (new modules, refactors, deletes) and before running tests or claiming done
instincts.action: Run `uv run basedpyright` then `uv run ruff check .` and fix all issues before `uv run pytest`.

instincts.title: Prefer UX Tests Over Internal Coupling
instincts.trigger: When adding tests for command behavior
instincts.action: Test through the CLI boundary (command invocation) rather than deep internal functions; assert the minimal observable behavior that matters (selected lines, not full dumps).

instincts.title: After deleting a module, systematically eliminate all references
instincts.trigger: A refactor removes/renames modules (large deletions or package reshapes)
instincts.action: Search for old module names and key symbols (e.g. with ripgrep/grep), update imports, docs, and tests together; ensure no dead entrypoints remain.

instincts.title: Remove Orphaned Package Surface After Large Deletions
instincts.trigger: When removing many files or an entire subtree (e.g., `.../poly/*`)
instincts.action: Check `__init__.py` exports, public module surface, and docs/README links for orphans; ensure imports fail nowhere and no dead entrypoints remain.

instincts.title: Stabilize CLI Output Contracts
instincts.trigger: When printing structured lists/summaries in CLI
instincts.action: Ensure ordering and formatting are deterministic (sorting, fixed labels); avoid including volatile fields in user-facing output unless explicitly requested, and ensure tests lock the contract.

instincts.title: Subsystem Removal Requires Repo-Wide Sweep
instincts.trigger: When deleting or deprecating a substantial module/package (especially with many submodules)
instincts.action: Immediately sweep for imports/exports, docs references, CLI help/examples, tests, and any API schemas; remove or rewrite references so the repo remains coherent and discoverable.

instincts.title: Use repo-root-relative paths in template docs
instincts.trigger: When editing README/docs content that will be scaffolded into a repo (e.g., src/agent_loom/compound/opencode/README.md, src/agent_loom/compound/opencode/.loom/compound/README.md, `.opencode/commands/*.md`).
instincts.action: Write links and references using repo-root-relative paths (no absolute machine paths, no editor URIs) and avoid references to removed/retired template files (e.g., AGENTS.md copies) to keep docs portable and deterministic.
